title: orobanche ludoviciana subsp. multiflora
intro: Orobanche ludoviciana subsp. multiflora is a parasitic plant species also known as the "Indian broomrape" or "Ludoviciana broomrape", belonging to the Orobanchaceae family. Traditionally, Orobanche ludoviciana subsp. multiflora has been used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to offer several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and aiding in the treatment of various respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, Orobanche ludoviciana subsp. multiflora is often used in the form of decoctions or infusions, and it can also be dried and used as a tea or added to other medicinal blends.
